summary: Set 052 Session 3 of 3 (final) - Docs, UAT, version bump, cross-provider verification, close-out. This closes the cost-metrics icon redesign; the set's top-level status flips to complete. Docs reconciled: docs/repository-reference.md (CostDashboard.ts row) + the extension README.md now describe the new behavior (router-capability gating absent on Lightweight, on-open staleness prompt, three honest states); confirmed no live human-facing doc referenced the fictional config.py METRICS_ENABLED flag (S2 had already removed it from webview/dashboard.html) and that the read path was already documented as router-metrics.jsonl in adoption-bootstrap.md. UAT checklist (elected at S1): 052-cost-metrics-icon-redesign-uat-checklist.json, 16 items - programmatically-verifiable items (read-path resolution, staleness predicate, honest-copy invariants, state selection, gate wiring) marked complete with the covering test; live-VS-Code items (icon present/absent, painted banner, editor reveal on the update action) left Passes=false/pending for manual operator UAT post-publish - precisely the coverage the S2 D7 Playwright->manifest-guard pivot deferred. Version bump: extension 0.26.1 -> 0.27.0 (package.json + package-lock.json both version nodes, CHANGELOG.md 0.27.0 entry, CLAUDE.md version-walk cascade noting the interim 0.26.1 icon patch is superseded); TS-only, no companion PyPI release (ai_router untouched). Cross-provider verification of the S2 implementation by gemini-2.5-pro (different provider from the Claude orchestrator), fed the actual code (routerConfig.ts, dashboardHtml.ts, metrics.ts, costDashboardGate.test.ts, the extension.ts gate fn + package.json gate wiring) plus neutral framing of the two flagged deviations via run_s3_verification.py: verdict VERIFIED_WITH_NOTES, 0 critical / 0 blocking, BOTH deviations APPROVED (D7 Playwright->manifest-guard pivot judged superior to the brittle E2E smoke; schema reconciliation session_num->session_number + adjudication-row filtering judged a necessary part of a complete fix, not scope creep). The two 'important' verifier entries were unsolicited praise (good test pattern, defensive YAML-parse handling), not defects. One nice-to-have (a YYYY-MM-DD date-format clarification comment in computeStaleness) addressed in-flight - the sole S3 code change to S2's implementation, comment-only and behavior-preserving. change-log.md + s3-close-reason.md + s3-verification.md authored. tsc --noEmit clean; npm run test:unit 584 passing / 2 failing (the 2 are the known pre-existing Set-026 stub-harness failures in untouched files - unchanged from S2's count). Marketplace vsix-v0.27.0 publish HELD for operator-initiated tag-push (confirm VSCE_PAT freshness first - it expired during the 0.24.0 publish). Routed verification cost $0.017121; cumulative Set 052 $0.065548 of $10 NTE (0.66%).

reason.specifics: Set 052 (3 of 3) is complete - the cost-metrics icon redesign shipped end-to-end (read-path root-cause fix + router-capability tier gate + in-extension staleness banner + three honest dashboard states), version-bumped to extension 0.27.0 (Marketplace-only; publish held for operator tag-push), UAT checklist compiled, and the S2 implementation cross-provider verified (VERIFIED_WITH_NOTES, both deviations approved). No next session in this set. No follow-on orchestrator action required beyond the operator pushing the vsix-v0.27.0 tag after confirming VSCE_PAT freshness.
